evidence.detail: router1: interface Serial0/1/1 neigh 3.3.3.3 addr 10.0.0.9 state ospf-nbr-full (from get_ospf router1)
router3: interface Serial0/1/0 neigh 1.1.1.1 addr 10.0.0.10 state ospf-nbr-full (from get_ospf router3)
router2: interface Serial0/1/1 neigh 3.3.3.3 addr 10.0.0.6 state ospf-nbr-full (from get_ospf router2)
router3: interface Serial0/1/1 neigh 2.2.2.2 addr 10.0.0.5 state ospf-nbr-full (from get_ospf router3)
router1: interface Serial0/1/0 neigh 2.2.2.2 addr 10.0.0.2 state ospf-nbr-init (only seen from router1; router2 shows no neighbor on Serial0/1/0) (from get_ospf router1)

recent_changes.summary: Added devices router1, router2, router3 with OSPF process and router-ids from recent get_ospf scans.
Discovered/updated OSPF adjacencies: router1<->router3 (S0/1/1<->S0/1/0), router2<->router3 (S0/1/1<->S0/1/1), router1<->router2 (S0/1/0<->S0/1/0 - one-sided observed).

router1 interface GigabitEthernet0/0/0 state DR cost 1
router2 ospf.router_id = 2.2.2.2
router2 interface Serial0/1/1 state Point-to-Point cost 50 (neighbor 3.3.3.3 addr 10.0.0.6)
router2 interface Serial0/1/0 state Point-to-Point cost 50
router2 interface GigabitEthernet0/0/0 state DR cost 1
router3 ospf.router_id = 3.3.3.3
router3 interface Loopback1 state Loopback cost 1

router3 interface Serial0/1/0 state Point-to-Point cost 50 (neighbor 1.1.1.1 addr 10.0.0.10)

router3: interface Serial0/1/0 neigh 1.1.1.1 addr 10.0.0.10 state ospf-nbr-full (from get_ospf router3)

router3: interface Serial0/1/1 neigh 2.2.2.2 addr 10.0.0.5 state ospf-nbr-full (from get_ospf router3)
router1: interface Serial0/1/0 neigh 2.2.2.2 addr 10.0.0.2 state ospf-nbr-init (only seen from router1; router2 shows no neighbor on Serial0/1/0) (from get_ospf router1)

router1 interface Serial0/1/1 state Point-to-Point cost 49

router3 interface Serial0/1/0 state Point-to-Point cost 50 (neighbor 1.1.1.1 addr 10.0.0.10)

root_causes.type: Asymmetric OSPF hello reception
root_causes.cause: R1 is receiving OSPF hellos from R2 (R1 shows neighbor in INIT) but R2 reports no neighbor on the same interface — R2 is not receiving or not processing R1's hellos, preventing adjacency from progressing past INIT.
evidence: router1 (Serial0/1/0): neighbor 2.2.2.2 addr 10.0.0.2 state ospf-nbr-init (seen from get_ospf(router1))
router2 (Serial0/1/0): interface state Point-to-Point, neighbors: [] (seen from get_ospf(router2))
Topology note: this is one-sided — only router1 reports the neighbor; router2 does not.

root_causes.type: Packet filtering / ACL blocking OSPF hellos toward R2
root_causes.cause: An inbound ACL, firewall, or packet filter on/near router2 Serial0/1/0 could be dropping OSPF (protocol 89) packets from R1 so R2 never sees R1's hellos; OSPF otherwise appears functional on R2 (adjacency to R3 is full).
evidence: router2 (Serial0/1/1) neigh 3.3.3.3 addr 10.0.0.6 state ospf-nbr-full (OSPF works toward R3)
router2 (Serial0/1/0) neighbors: [] while router1 reports INIT for 2.2.2.2 on the same link

root_causes.type: Interface/IP or Layer‑2 misconfiguration on the R1<->R2 link
root_causes.cause: IP addressing, subnet/mask mismatch or an L2 issue on the Serial0/1/0 link could prevent proper hello exchange reception/processing on R2 (one-sided adjacency).
evidence: router1 shows neighbor address 10.0.0.2 for 2.2.2.2 (R1 sees something at that address) but router2 shows no OSPF neighbor on its Serial0/1/0
router2's other OSPF adjacencies are healthy (adjacency to R3 is full), pointing to a link-specific problem.

root_causes.type: OSPF parameter mismatch (timers or authentication)
root_causes.cause: Different OSPF hello/dead timers or mismatched authentication on the Serial0/1/0 interfaces could prevent adjacency formation; R1's INIT state is consistent with R2 not accepting/processing R1's hellos due to parameter mismatch.
evidence: router1 (Serial0/1/0) neighbor state: ospf-nbr-init toward 2.2.2.2
router2 (Serial0/1/0) reports no neighbor at all — symptomatic of a config mismatch preventing mutual recognition

missing_info: show ip ospf interface Serial0/1/0 on router1 and router2 (to compare hello/dead timers, network type, authentication)
show running-config interface Serial0/1/0 on router2 (IP address/mask, ACLs applied, OSPF authentication or passive setting)
show interface Serial0/1/0 on router1 and router2 (line protocol, CRC/errors, input/output packet counters)
show access-lists (or show run | include access-list) on router2 to identify any ACLs that could block protocol 89

data.intent_description: OSPF adjacency on router2 (Serial0/1/0) is not forming: R1 reports neighbor in INIT while R2 shows no neighbor on that interface. Restore bidirectional OSPF adjacency so the neighbor on Serial0/1/0 reaches FULL.

plan.problem: One-sided OSPF adjacency on router2 Serial0/1/0 (R2 not receiving or processing R1's OSPF hellos).
plan.fix_summary: Allow OSPF packets to reach router2 on Serial0/1/0, ensure interface IP/subnet and L2 are correct, and align OSPF parameters so adjacency can form.

parameters: permit protocol 89 any any inbound on interface Serial0/1/0
remove or adjust any ACL entry on Serial0/1/0 that denies IP protocol 89
ensure interface IP and subnet match R1 on the link (place in same /30 as R1's neighbor 10.0.0.2); correct IP/mask if mismatched
set OSPF hello/dead timers and authentication on Serial0/1/0 to match R1 (use router1 values from show ip ospf interface)
remove passive-interface on Serial0/1/0 if set

root_causes.type: OSPF parameter mismatch (timers or authentication)
root_causes.cause: Different OSPF hello/dead timers or mismatched authentication on the Serial0/1/0 interfaces could prevent adjacency formation; R1's INIT state is consistent with R2 not accepting/processing R1's hellos due to parameter mismatch.
evidence: router1 (Serial0/1/0) neighbor state: ospf-nbr-init toward 2.2.2.2
router2 (Serial0/1/0) reports no neighbor at all — symptomatic of a config mismatch preventing mutual recognition

missing_info: show ip ospf interface Serial0/1/0 on router1 and router2 (to compare hello/dead timers, network type, authentication)
show running-config interface Serial0/1/0 on router2 (IP address/mask, ACLs applied, OSPF authentication or passive setting)
show interface Serial0/1/0 on router1 and router2 (line protocol, CRC/errors, input/output packet counters)
show access-lists (or show run | include access-list) on router2 to identify any ACLs that could block protocol 89

parameters: ensure interface IP and subnet match R1 on the link (place in same /30 as R1's neighbor 10.0.0.2); correct IP/mask if mismatched

plan.problem: One-sided OSPF adjacency on router2 Serial0/1/0 (R2 not receiving or processing R1's OSPF hellos).
plan.fix_summary: Allow OSPF packets to reach router2 on Serial0/1/0, ensure interface IP/subnet and L2 are correct, and align OSPF parameters so adjacency can form.

parameters: permit protocol 89 any any inbound on interface Serial0/1/0
remove or adjust any ACL entry on Serial0/1/0 that denies IP protocol 89
ensure interface IP and subnet match R1 on the link (place in same /30 as R1's neighbor 10.0.0.2); correct IP/mask if mismatched
set OSPF hello/dead timers and authentication on Serial0/1/0 to match R1 (use router1 values from show ip ospf interface)
remove passive-interface on Serial0/1/0 if set

data.alert_description: OSPF adjacency on router2 (Serial0/1/0) is not forming: R1 reports neighbor in INIT while R2 shows no neighbor on that interface. Restore bidirectional OSPF adjacency so the neighbor on Serial0/1/0 reaches FULL.

root_causes.type: Asymmetric OSPF hello reception
root_causes.cause: R1 is receiving OSPF hellos from R2 (R1 shows neighbor in INIT) but R2 reports no neighbor on the same interface — R2 is not receiving or not processing R1's hellos, preventing adjacency from progressing past INIT.

root_causes.type: Packet filtering / ACL blocking OSPF hellos toward R2
root_causes.cause: An inbound ACL, firewall, or packet filter on/near router2 Serial0/1/0 could be dropping OSPF (protocol 89) packets from R1 so R2 never sees R1's hellos; OSPF otherwise appears functional on R2 (adjacency to R3 is full).

router2 (Serial0/1/0) neighbors: [] while router1 reports INIT for 2.2.2.2 on the same link

messages.content:
What was observed
- One-sided OSPF adjacency on the R1<->R2 Serial0/1/0 link: R1 showed R2 in INIT, while R2 reported no neighbor on Serial0/1/0.

Diagnosis (main cause)
- R2 was not receiving/processing R1's OSPF hellos (asymmetric hello reception) — most likely due to an inbound packet filter or incorrect interface/IP on R2’s Serial0/1/0.

Changes made
- Applied inbound ACL "ACL_ALLOW_OSPF" on router2 Serial0/1/0 to allow OSPF (protocol 89).
- Configured Serial0/1/0 on router2 with IP 10.0.0.2/255.255.255.252.
- Ensured OSPF process 1 is configured with router-id 2.2.2.2.

Verification
- Passed. Evidence: router2 now shows Serial0/1/0 neighbor 1.1.1.1 (10.0.0.1) state ospf-nbr-full; interface is up with zero input/output errors; running-config shows the IP and ACL applied.
- Remaining issues: none. Adjacency restored to FULL.
